title: Lagascea pteropoda
taxonomy: Lagascea pteropoda is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. It belongs to the genus Lagascea, which consists of various species of annual herbs. The plant is native to South America, specifically in the mountainous regions of Peru and Chile. Its classification also places it within the tribe Heliantheae, a large and diverse group of flowering plants. Lagascea pteropoda is an annual or short-lived perennial plant with unique characteristics that distinguish it from other species within its genus.
uses: Lagascea pteropoda, also known as the Peruvian Flannel Flower or South American Flannel Flower, is used in traditional medicine. Its medicinal uses include being an anti-inflammatory agent, as well as possessing antimicrobial properties.
